steps.title: Rewrite the given equation
steps.content: The equation $\left(\dfrac{1}{2}x - \dfrac{41}{2}\right)^2 - 64 = 0$ can be written as $\left(\dfrac{x-41}{2}\right)^2 = 64$, since $\dfrac{1}{2}x - \dfrac{41}{2} = \dfrac{x-41}{2}$.

steps.title: Isolate $(x-41)^2$
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $4$: $(x-41)^2 = 4 \cdot 64 = 256$.

  • Choice A — $256$: matches $(x-41)^2 = 256$. ✓
  • Choice B — $128$: would require multiplying by $2$, not $4$. ✗
  • Choice C — $41$: confuses the constant in the expression for the answer. ✗
  • Choice D — $8$: confuses $\sqrt{64}$ for the answer instead of $4 \times 64$. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
